name: 45 · Live fMP4 Stream (MSE render adapter)
description:
The presentation-side counterpart to the wire-transport demos: it proves the UI's render-adapter registry dispatches a data channel to the RIGHT player off its element content_type, exactly as the executors dispatch the byte transport off the `open` descriptor (docs/25 §6). This is the container-codec sibling of demo 42 (raw-PCM live audio): where 42 emits headerless `audio/L16` the browser plays through Web Audio, this emits `audio/mp4;codecs="mp4a.40.2"` — a FRAGMENTED MP4 (`ftyp` + `moov`(mvex) init segment, then `moof` + `mdat` media fragments) — which `planLiveRender` routes to the Media Source Extensions player instead.

The producer synthesizes an A-major arpeggio and muxes it with PyAV (which bundles libav — no system ffmpeg) into a fragmented MP4, pacing the fragments out over the Data/Out channel `media` in ~real time. A validator drains the channel transport-unaware (`for chunk in stream('media')`) and parses the received bytes' MP4 box structure to assert they are a genuine, multi-fragment, MSE-appendable stream — the wire-level half of a verification the browser can't automate. Because the JetStream transport is durable + ordered, the received byte count equals the produced one.

Start → producer (PyAV fragmented MP4 over `media`) → validator (parse boxes off the wire) → End. Run: `mekhan run 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000450 --start-tokens '{}'` (no inputs). To see the MSE player: while the instance runs, open it in the app, find the `media` channel, and hit Play live — the arpeggio decodes and plays through an `<audio>` element as the fragments land.
